title: The Bitbrain Open Access Sleep (BOAS) dataset
description: The Bitbrain Open Access Sleep (BOAS) dataset comprises 128 nights of simultaneous polysomnography and wearable EEG recordings designed to validate emerging wearable sleep monitoring technologies against clinical gold-standard systems. The dataset includes comprehensive PSG recordings from a Micromed Brain Quick Plus Evolution system and forehead EEG data from a Bitbrain wearable headband, along with human expert consensus sleep stage labels and AI-generated sleep scoring annotations. This resource enables direct comparison and validation of wearable EEG performance for sleep stage classification in a diverse population sample.
methods_description: Data were collected from 128 nights of simultaneous recording using two systems: a Micromed Brain Quick Plus Evolution PSG system (recording EEG at F3, F4, C3, C4, O1, O2; EOG; chin EMG; abdominal and thoracic breathing belts; thermistor and nasal cannula airflow; and PPG) and a Bitbrain wearable EEG headband (recording forehead EEG at AF7/AF8 locations, 6-axis IMU, and PPG). Sleep stages were independently scored by three expert sleep scorers following AASM criteria, with consensus labels derived when at least two scorers agreed. A deep learning model was trained on consensus labels using cross-validation, achieving 87.13% accuracy on PSG data and 86.71% on wearable EEG data.

funding_references.funder_name: Programa Misiones de I+D en Inteligencia Artificial
